column2: What makes a device a multifunction device in Objective 3.7?
column4: It combines printer functions with other capabilities such as scanning, faxing, network connectivity, phone-line connectivity, or web-based printing.
column5:
Why

Multifunction devices require more deployment planning than a simple single-purpose printer because several services may need power, network, phone, driver, and access configuration.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.41; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 3.7
column10: A+::220-1201::3.7 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::MultifunctionDevicesandPrinters Basic Printer MFD Source::Messer-v170

column2: Before unboxing a large shared multifunction printer, what should a technician verify about the setup location?
column4: Verify that the location has enough space, safe walkways, power, network access, and accessibility for users.
column5:
Why

Deployment problems often start before configuration. A device that blocks walkways or lacks power/network access will not be usable even with the correct driver.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.41; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 3.7
column10: A+::220-1201::3.7 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::MultifunctionDevicesandPrinters Basic Printer Deployment Scenario Source::Messer-v170

column2: A new printer is installed, but users cannot see options for the device's supported trays or duplex settings. What should be checked first?
column3: The operating system exposes printer features through software matched to that model.
column4: Check that the correct printer driver is installed for the printer model and operating system.
column5:
Why

The driver tells the operating system which printer features are available. The wrong driver can hide trays, duplex options, or language options even when the printer is physically installed correctly.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.41; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 3.7
column10: A+::220-1201::3.7 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::MultifunctionDevicesandPrinters Basic HighYield Printer Driver Scenario Source::Messer-v170

column2: Why must the printer driver language match the printer's supported language, such as PCL or PostScript?
column4: The printer must be able to interpret the page description language sent by the driver; a mismatch can produce garbled or unexpected output.
column5:
Distinction

This is a configuration match, not a network-cabling problem. If data reaches the printer but the language is wrong, the device may print unusable pages.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.41; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 3.7
column10: A+::220-1201::3.7 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::MultifunctionDevicesandPrinters Basic HighYield Printer Driver PCL PostScript Source::Messer-v170

column2: What role does firmware play in a multifunction printer?
column4: Firmware is the device's internal software that starts the system, connects to the network, interprets incoming data, and runs the print process.
column5:
Why

Firmware is updated for compatibility and bug fixes, but the update should use the vendor's supported process from a stable platform and power source.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.42; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 3.7
column10: A+::220-1201::3.7 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::MultifunctionDevicesandPrinters Basic HighYield Printer Firmware Source::Messer-v170

column2: When is USB the appropriate printer connectivity choice?
column4: Use USB for a direct local connection between the printer and a computer.
column5:
Distinction

USB is simple for one directly connected computer. If many users need access, a network connection, printer share, or print server is usually the more relevant deployment model.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.42; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 3.7
column10: A+::220-1201::3.7 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::MultifunctionDevicesandPrinters Basic Printer USB Source::Messer-v170

column2: When is Ethernet the appropriate printer connectivity choice?
column4: Use Ethernet when the printer should connect directly to the wired network with an RJ45 network connection.
column5:
Why

Ethernet lets the printer operate as a network device instead of depending on one attached workstation's USB connection.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.42; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 3.7
column10: A+::220-1201::3.7 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::MultifunctionDevicesandPrinters Basic Printer Ethernet Source::Messer-v170

column2: What is the practical difference between 802.11 infrastructure mode and ad hoc mode for printer sharing?
column4: Infrastructure mode uses an access point for network access, while ad hoc mode creates a direct wireless link without an access point.
column5:
Distinction

Bluetooth is also wireless, but it is short-range. For normal network printing, infrastructure Wi-Fi is usually the more scalable wireless model.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.42; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 3.7
column10: A+::220-1201::3.7 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::MultifunctionDevicesandPrinters Basic Printer Wireless Source::Messer-v170

column2: What is the key deployment difference between a printer share and a print server?
column4: A printer share depends on the computer connected to the printer sharing it, while a print server manages jobs for a shared printer without relying on a user's workstation as the sharing point.
column5:
Why

With a printer share, the sharing computer must be running. A print-server deployment centralizes queue and job management for shared access.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.42; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 3.7
column10: A+::220-1201::3.7 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::MultifunctionDevicesandPrinters Basic HighYield Printer Sharing PrintServer Source::Messer-v170

column2: Which printer setting should be enabled when a user wants output on both sides of each page?
column4: Enable duplex printing, assuming the printer supports it.
column5:
Distinction

Duplex is about two-sided output. It is separate from orientation, tray selection, or quality.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.42; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 3.7
column10: A+::220-1201::3.7 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::MultifunctionDevicesandPrinters Basic HighYield Printer Duplex Source::Messer-v170

column2: Which printer setting controls portrait versus landscape output?
column5:
Distinction

Orientation changes how content is laid out on the page. It does not physically rotate the paper; the printer compensates during output.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.42; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 3.7
column10: A+::220-1201::3.7 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::MultifunctionDevicesandPrinters Basic Printer Orientation Source::Messer-v170

column2: A user needs a job to print on company letterhead loaded in a specific tray. Which configuration setting matters most?
column4: Tray settings; select the tray that contains the letterhead or required paper.
column5:
Why

Tray settings map the print job to the correct paper source. This is separate from duplex, orientation, and quality settings.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.42; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 3.7
column10: A+::220-1201::3.7 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::MultifunctionDevicesandPrinters Basic HighYield Printer Tray Scenario Source::Messer-v170

column2: Which printer setting should be adjusted when a user needs to change resolution, color, greyscale, or color-saving behavior?
column5:
Distinction

Quality settings affect how output is rendered. They do not choose the paper tray, page orientation, or two-sided printing.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.42; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 3.7
column10: A+::220-1201::3.7 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::MultifunctionDevicesandPrinters Basic Printer Quality Source::Messer-v170

column2: Why configure user authentication and permissions on a shared printer?
column4: They control who can print and who can manage the printer.
column5:
Distinction

Printing rights and management rights are not the same. A normal user may be allowed to print without being allowed to change device settings.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.42; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 3.7
column10: A+::220-1201::3.7 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::MultifunctionDevicesandPrinters Basic Printer Security Source::Messer-v170

column2: Which printer security feature helps prevent confidential jobs from sitting unattended in the output tray?
column4: Use secured print or badge release so the job prints only after the user authenticates at the device.
column5:
Why

Secured print commonly uses a passcode at the printer. Badging uses the employee badge at the device. Both delay release until the user is present.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.42; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 3.7
column10: A+::220-1201::3.7 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::MultifunctionDevicesandPrinters Basic HighYield Printer Security SecuredPrint Source::Messer-v170

column2: What is the purpose of audit logs on a public or shared printer?
column4: Audit logs help track printer usage, costs, and security-related events.
column5:
Why

Logging may be available from the printer, print server, or operating system events. The key idea is accountability for shared-device activity.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.42; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 3.7
column10: A+::220-1201::3.7 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::MultifunctionDevicesandPrinters Basic Printer Security Logging Source::Messer-v170

column2: How should a technician choose between scan to email, scan to SMB, and scan to cloud?
column4: Choose the destination that matches the workflow: email sends scans to an inbox, SMB sends scans to a network share, and cloud services send scans to cloud storage.
column5:
Distinction

Large scans can fill mailboxes. SMB is useful for Microsoft-style file shares, while cloud scanning fits workflows that already use cloud storage.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.42; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 3.7
column10: A+::220-1201::3.7 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::MultifunctionDevicesandPrinters Basic HighYield Printer Scanner SMB Cloud Source::Messer-v170

column2: When should a user choose an automatic document feeder instead of a flatbed scanner?
column4: Choose an ADF for scanning multiple pages automatically; use the flatbed for single pages or items that need to lie flat.
column5:
Distinction

The ADF feeds pages through the scanner. The flatbed is better for one-off pages, fragile originals, or items that should not be fed through rollers.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.42; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 3.7
column10: A+::220-1201::3.7 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::MultifunctionDevicesandPrinters Basic HighYield Printer Scanner ADF Source::Messer-v170
